metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_3840
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.6: "Neto2 Ser-409 phosphorylation inhibits synaptic targeting of GluK1 in CA1 pyramidal neurons"
- "As previously reported, we found a substantial increase in GluK1 synaptic responses in the presence of Neto2 WT (Fig. 6A). Co-expression of GluK1 with the phosphodeficient mutant Neto2 S409A also showed a similar increase in synaptic responses (Fig. 6B). By contrast, there was significantly less enhancement of synaptic currents when co-expressed with Neto2 S409D, the phosphomimetic mutant (Fig. 6, C and D). We have previously shown that Neto2 itself does not regulate AMPAR-mediated synaptic transmission (9). Taken together, these results show that when Neto2 is phosphorylated at Ser-409 the synaptic targeting of GluK1 is inhibited.". Experimental description: - "rat hippocampal slice cultures were biolistically transfected "
- Species of over-expressed Neto2 is not stated in the publication. The authors were contacted for additional information (dd 2/7/2018, rochek@ninds.nih.gov). Answer:

"Dear Pim van Nierop,

Here is the species information for all the constructs used/ experiments conducted. Hope this is the information you are looking for. Please let me know if you have any other questions.

Fig. 1, 2 and 3
GST-Neto1 (C-tail)- Mouse
GST-Neto2 (C-tail)- Rat

Fig. 4A-4D
Overexpression of Neto2 in heterologous cells- Rat

Fig. 4E
Detection of endogenous phosphorylation from mouse brain- no overexpression

Fig. 5 and 6
Overexpression of Neto2 and mutants- Rat

Best,
Richa Lomash."
